explanation: Pinging the gateway's IP sends an ICMP echo request to it; a reply proves the host can reach the gateway at both link and IP layer (ARP resolved, subnet mask and gateway setting correct). Note the source scan preserved only one option for this item, so there are no distractors to weigh.

IP addresses are hierarchical: the address splits into a network prefix plus a host part, so routers can aggregate many hosts under one prefix and forward on the prefix alone. Ethernet/MAC addresses are flat (vendor OUI plus serial number, no location information), so they cannot be summarized and are only usable inside one broadcast domain.

A receive equalizer is a transversal filter built from a tapped delay line: it takes copies of the signal delayed by successive tap intervals, weights them and sums them so the echoes cancel the intersymbol interference caused by delay distortion. The distractors are unrelated modem parts: a gearshift is speed fallback, a descrambler undoes scrambling, and a difference engine is a mechanical calculator.

Answer unverified: this item is quarantined as unanswerable. Only one option survived the scan, 'Ethernet topology', which is a fragment of the question rather than an answer, and it is wrong on the facts: FDDI is a dual counter-rotating token-passing fiber ring used for campus and MAN backbones, not an Ethernet topology. The intact version of this question asks which topology FDDI uses, and the answer there is ring.

Page traffic is the rate at which pages are moved in and out of main memory (page-ins and page-outs), which is why excessive page traffic is the symptom of thrashing. Option D describes the resident set (how many pages are currently loaded), a static count, not the movement.

Answer unverified: the source key marks D (Blocked), but Blocked/waiting IS one of the fundamental process states (new, ready, running, blocked, terminated) and the keyed option even concedes it by adding 'can be substituted with wait'. The genuine odd one out is B, 'Executed', because the actual state is 'running/executing'; review panel derived B as the correct answer.

LRU stands for Least Recently Used: it evicts the page whose most recent reference is furthest in the past, using locality of reference as a proxy for which page will be needed last. The other three expansions are invented terms.

The whole IEEE 802.11 WLAN family, including 802.11a, is branded Wi-Fi. Ethernet is 802.3 and WiMAX is 802.16. The trap is D: 802.11a is a different PHY from 802.11g (5 GHz versus 2.4 GHz, though both reach 54 Mbps), so they are not other names for each other.

Answer unverified: the source PDF carried no key for this item and only two options survived; the panel derived B. The kernel is kept memory-resident because it is the most frequently called code in the system, servicing every system call, interrupt and context switch, so paging it out would fault on nearly every dispatch. Managing interrupt calls (A) is something the kernel does, not the reason it must stay in primary memory.

== is a reference (identity) comparison: it asks whether two variables hold the same address, and for primitives whether the raw values match. .equals() compares content according to the override in the class. Classic trap: new String("ab") == new String("ab") is false because they are two objects, while .equals() on them is true.
NAND is NOT(AND), so the output is low only when the AND condition is satisfied, i.e. both inputs are 1: 1 NAND 1 = 0. Inputs 00, 01 and 10 all give an AND of 0, so the inverted output is 1 (high).

Algorithm efficiency is measured on two axes: time complexity (how many operations as a function of input size) and space complexity (how much extra memory). Processor and memory are the hardware resources, not the measures of the algorithm itself.

The question asks which statement is FALSE. In C++, cin is an object of the class istream, tied to standard input; it is not itself a class, so C reverses the relationship and is the false statement. A, B and D are all true descriptions of cin.

Constructors can be overloaded freely, so a class may declare any number of them as long as each has a distinct parameter signature. If you write none at all, the compiler supplies a default constructor, so the count is never actually forced to be 0, 1 or 2.

A schema is the complete logical description of a database, so it covers all three: the data elements (fields and their types), the records and files they are grouped into, and the relationships between records. Hence 'all of the above'.

Boehm's spiral model is the risk-driven process model: every loop of the spiral contains an explicit risk-analysis quadrant where prototypes are built to retire the biggest risks before committing to the next increment. Waterfall is strictly sequential with no risk loop, and agile and rapid prototyping manage change and requirements rather than risk analysis as such.

DDCMP is byte-count (character-oriented): a count field in the header states the data length, so the receiver can find message boundaries in software with no special framing hardware. SDLC is bit-oriented and relies on hardware detection of the flag pattern 01111110 plus bit de-stuffing to locate the start of a message. D is wrong because SDLC does use CRC.

A Mealy machine's output is a function of the present state AND the present inputs, so its output can change as soon as the input changes, within the clock cycle. A Moore machine's output is a function of the present state only, so it changes only after a clock edge. Consequence: Mealy usually needs fewer states and reacts one cycle earlier, but its output is more prone to glitches.

The truth table shows JK = 00 hold (Q stays), 01 reset (Q = 0), 10 set (Q = 1) and 11 toggle (Q = Q'), which is the JK flip-flop. The giveaway is the 11 row: an SR flip-flop treats 11 as invalid/forbidden, while JK defines it as a toggle.

Search, insert and delete in a BST follow a single root-to-leaf path with one comparison per level, so the cost is proportional to the height. In the best (balanced) case the height is log2(n), giving O(log n). The classic trap: a BST built from already sorted input degenerates into a linked list of height n, so the worst case is O(n).
